layman_headline: Crypto fear is high but no clear swing trade setup passes the quality bar right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with a score of 26/100, and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.3%, which means altcoins are getting crushed relative to Bitcoin. The swing trade candidates available all have significant problems — bearish chart structures, poor risk/reward ratios, or lack of reliable data. While some coins like GRASS and JTO have dropped sharply and could bounce, their charts show active selling pressure with no confirmed reversal yet. The best-performing asset in the top 20 is ZEC (+13.75% weekly), but it wasn't on the swing candidate list with proper analysis.
layman_action: Stay patient and keep your cash ready. The market is fearful and altcoins are weak — this is not the time to force a trade. Wait for a clearer setup with confirmed support and a bullish reversal signal.
expert_reasoning: Systematically evaluating all 9 candidates: USDG is a stablecoin (no trade). GRASS has a -0.90 chart bias with bearish engulfing and 0.48x R/R — avoid. VVV has zero chart data. XPL has a terrible 0.13x R/R ratio. JTO has the best case with a +0.40 chart bias and 1.26x R/R after a -20% pullback, but RSI at 59 is not oversold and BTC dominance at 56.3% makes alt longs risky. BONK has a -1.40 chart bias with bearish marubozu — strong avoid. ETHFI has a +0.60 chart bias but is already near resistance ($0.40) with only 0.11x R/R. MON and ZRO lack sufficient data. With the Fear & Greed at 26 and improving, a broader market bounce could come, but no individual setup has the confluence of oversold RSI + bullish chart + good R/R + catalyst that our reliable signals (volume_breakout, RSI bounce) require.
risk_factors: U.S.-Iran military strikes could escalate, driving risk-off sentiment across all crypto
BTC dominance at 56.3% and rising means capital is leaving altcoins — fighting this trend is dangerous
Fear & Greed at 26 could deteriorate further before any meaningful reversal
watch_list: BTC price action around $64K — a decisive break above $65K could lift alts, while a drop below $62K would signal more pain
Fear & Greed index trend — currently improving from 24 weekly avg to 26, watch for acceleration above 30
ZEC momentum — +13.75% weekly with rising volume ($276M) could be the first alt to break out if trend continues
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear-driven environment with BTC flat near $64K and high dominance squeezing altcoins. Geopolitical tensions (U.S.-Iran strikes) and stalling crypto IPOs add to the cautious tone, though some analysts note BTC may be in late-stage bear market territory.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and fearful sentiment create a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades — patience is the highest-conviction play.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 (Fear) with improving trend from 24 avg — market is fearful but not yet at extreme levels that reliably trigger bounces.
No candidate has RSI below 35 (true oversold). Best candidates BONK (40) and MON (41) are approaching but not confirmed oversold, reducing bounce probability.
U.S. launching fresh Iran strikes creates geopolitical risk-off pressure; crypto IPO market stalling signals institutional caution.
Bitcoin described as 'nearing late stages of bear market' by Jamie Coutts — suggests macro bottom may be forming but not confirmed yet.
ETH derivatives reset noted alongside tokenization boom narrative — could set up ETH-adjacent plays once fear subsides.
